### Logical Database Design Anti-Patterns

  * [Multi-Valued Attribute](https://github.com/jarulraj/sqlcheck/blob/master/docs/logical/1001.md)
  * [Recursive Dependency](https://github.com/jarulraj/sqlcheck/blob/master/docs/logical/1002.md)
  * [Primary Key Does Not Exist](https://github.com/jarulraj/sqlcheck/blob/master/docs/logical/1003.md)

dc.l	DINNER,5001
	dc.l	BREAKFAST,1000
	dc.l	BREAKFAST,1001
	dc.l	CAR_RENTAL,4
	dc.l	0,0

type(1) = DINNER: amount(1) = 5001
type(2) = BREAKFAST: amount(2) = 1000
type(3) = BREAKFAST: amount(3) = 1001
type(4) = CARRENTAL: amount(4) = 4
CALL PrintExpenses(type(), amount())

40 TYPE(1) = DINNER: AMOUNT(1) = 5001
50 TYPE(2) = BREAKFAST: AMOUNT(2) = 1000
60 TYPE(3) = BREAKFAST: AMOUNT(3) = 1001
70 TYPE(4) = CARRENTAL: AMOUNT(4) = 4
80 GOSUB 100

40 type[1] = DINNER: amount[1] = 5001
50 type[2] = BREAKFAST: amount[2] = 1000
60 type[3] = BREAKFAST: amount[3] = 1001
70 type[4] = CARRENTAL: amount[4] = 4
80 GOSUB 100

MOVE 5001 TO WS-AMOUNT(2)
           MOVE 1000 TO WS-AMOUNT(3)
           MOVE 1001 TO WS-AMOUNT(4)
           MOVE 4 TO WS-AMOUNT(5)
           PERFORM PRINTREPORT

expenses(1)%amount = 1000
        expenses(2)%type = BREAKFAST
        expenses(2)%amount = 1001
        expenses(3)%type = DINNER
        expenses(3)%amount = 5000

echo "Total expenses: ", total

printReport(@[(expenseType: DINNER, amount: 5000), (expenseType: DINNER, amount: 5001), (expenseType: BREAKFAST, amount: 1000), (expenseType: BREAKFAST, amount: 1001), (expenseType: CAR_RENTAL, amount: 4)])

}

printReport(new Expense(CAR_RENTAL, 5), new Expense(DINNER, 5000), new Expense(BREAKFAST, 1001), new Expense(CAR_RENTAL, 4));

expense.1.type = DINNER; expense.1.amount = 5001
expense.2.type = BREAKFAST; expense.2.amount = 1000
expense.3.type = BREAKFAST; expense.3.amount = 1001
expense.4.type = CAR_RENTAL; expense.4.amount = 4

expense2 := Expense type: 'Dinner' amount: 5001.
expense3 := Expense type: 'Breakfast' amount: 1000.
expense4 := Expense type: 'Breakfast' amount: 1001.
expense5 := Expense type: 'Car Rental' amount: 4.

type(1) = DINNER: amount(1) = 5001
type(2) = BREAKFAST: amount(2) = 1000
type(3) = BREAKFAST: amount(3) = 1001
type(4) = CARRENTAL: amount(4) = 4
htmlMode = 1

40 TYPE(1) = DINNER: AMOUNT(1) = 5001
50 TYPE(2) = BREAKFAST: AMOUNT(2) = 1000
60 TYPE(3) = BREAKFAST: AMOUNT(3) = 1001
70 TYPE(4) = CARRENTAL: AMOUNT(4) = 4
80 htmlMode = 1

printReport True ([
        Expense Breakfast 1000,
        Expense Breakfast 1001,
        Expense Dinner 5000,
        Expense Dinner 5001,

echo "</html>"

printReport(@[(expenseType: DINNER, amount: 5000), (expenseType: DINNER, amount: 5001), (expenseType: BREAKFAST, amount: 1000), (expenseType: BREAKFAST, amount: 1001), (expenseType: CAR_RENTAL, amount: 4)], true)

ExpenseReport::print_report(true, [
    new Expense(ExpenseType::BREAKFAST, 1000),
    new Expense(ExpenseType::BREAKFAST, 1001),
    new Expense(ExpenseType::DINNER, 5000),
    new Expense(ExpenseType::DINNER, 5001),

}

printReport(1, new Expense(BREAKFAST, 1000), new Expense(BREAKFAST, 1001), new Expense(DINNER, 5000),new Expense(DINNER, 5001),  new Expense(CAR_RENTAL, 4));

}

printReport(1, Expense.new(type => BREAKFAST, amount => 1000), Expense.new(type => BREAKFAST, amount => 1001), Expense.new(type => DINNER, amount => 5000), Expense.new(type => DINNER, amount => 5001), Expense.new(type => CAR_RENTAL, amount => 4));

}

printExpenses 1 [Expense new BREAKFAST 1000] [Expense new BREAKFAST 1001] [Expense new DINNER 5000] [Expense new DINNER 5001] [Expense new CAR_RENTAL 4]

Dim expenses As List(Of Expense) = new List(Of Expense)
        expenses.add(new Expense(ExpenseType.BREAKFAST, 1000))
        expenses.add(new Expense(ExpenseType.BREAKFAST, 1001))
        expenses.add(new Expense(ExpenseType.DINNER, 5000))
        expenses.add(new Expense(ExpenseType.DINNER, 5001))

&[
            Expense { type_: ExpenseType::Breakfast, amount: 1000 },
            Expense { type_: ExpenseType::Breakfast, amount: 1001 },
            Expense { type_: ExpenseType::Dinner, amount: 5000 },
            Expense { type_: ExpenseType::Dinner, amount: 5001 },

printReport(true, [
          new Expense("breakfast", 1000),
          new Expense("breakfast", 1001),
          new Expense("dinner", 5000),
          new Expense("dinner", 5001),

expenses: [
                Expense(type: ExpenseType.breakfast, amount: 1000),
                Expense(type: ExpenseType.breakfast, amount: 1001),
                Expense(type: ExpenseType.dinner, amount: 5000),
                Expense(type: ExpenseType.dinner, amount: 5001),

            new Expense(ExpenseType.DINNER, 5000),

I translated the original C# into a few other languages, (with a little help from my friends!), and slightly changed the starting position. This means I've actually done a small amount of refactoring already compared with the original form of the kata, and made it easier to get going with writing tests by giving you one failing unit test to start with. I also added test fixtures for Text-Based approval testing with TextTest (see [the TextTests](https://github.com/emilybache/GildedRose-Refactoring-Kata/tree/main/texttests))

As Bobby Johnson points out in his article "Why Most Solutions to Gilded Rose Miss The Bigger Picture" (on the Wayback Machine [here](https://web.archive.org/web/20230530152324/https://iamnotmyself.com/why-most-solutions-to-gilded-rose-miss-the-bigger-picture/)), it'll actually give you
better practice at handling a legacy code situation if you do this Kata in the original C#. However, I think this kata
is also really useful for practicing writing good tests using different frameworks and approaches, and the small changes I've made help with that. I think it's also interesting to compare what the refactored code and tests look like in different programming languages.

title: Indentation not consistent with previous lines
Function name not immediately followed by bracket
description: Function calls should have no space before opening bracket.

title: WITH clause closing bracket should be aligned with WITH keyword
description: Align closing bracket with WITH keyword.

title: Table aliases should be unique
description: Each table alias should be unique.

title: Ambiguous distinct
description: DISTINCT used with ambiguous column references.

title: Blank line expected but not found
Single whitespace expected after AS in WITH clause
Single whitespace expected after AS in FROM clause
Tables should not be aliased if not referenced
Inconsistent capitalisation of function names
description: Use consistent capitalization for function names.

title: Unqualified reference to column
description: Qualify column references with table alias.

title: Keywords should not be used as identifiers
Avoid table aliases in from clauses and join conditions
description: Use table names directly when possible.

title: Select wildcards then simple targets before calculations and aggregates
Do not use "SELECT DISTINCT" if it has no effect
Select targets should be on a new line unless there is only one select target
description: Format SELECT targets on separate lines.

title: Ambiguous ordering in ORDER BY clause
description: Use explicit column references in ORDER BY.

title: Inconsistent capitalisation of boolean/null literal
description: Use consistent capitalization for boolean/null literals.

title: SELECT clause should not contain unnecessary brackets
Join/From clauses should not contain subqueries
description: Avoid subqueries in JOIN/FROM when possible.

title: Case expression should have an ELSE clause
description: Add ELSE clause to CASE expressions.

title: Query produces an unknown number of result columns
description: Use explicit column lists instead of SELECT *.

title: Jinja tags should have a single whitespace on either side
Files should not end with a trailing newline
INNER JOIN must be fully qualified
Statements must end with a semicolon
Statements must not end with a semicolon
Use LEFT JOIN instead of RIGHT JOIN
description: Prefer LEFT JOIN over RIGHT JOIN.

title: Spaces within brackets
description: Do not use spaces within brackets.

title: Use NOT IN instead of NOT EXISTS
description: Prefer NOT IN over NOT EXISTS when appropriate.

title: Use NULL instead of empty string
